title: Display estimated ZIP-317 fee in /pay confirmation prompt
description:
What: Compute and display the estimated ZIP-317 transaction fee to the user when they type /pay in the chat UI, before the payment request is sent.

Algorithm:
1. In the /pay command handler in cli/src/commands.rs (near line 1264), after parsing amount_zatoshi, compute:
   - logical_actions = sapling_logical_actions(1, 2)  // 1 input assumed, 2 outputs (recipient + change)
   - estimated_fee_zatoshi = zip317_fee(logical_actions)
2. Display fee to user before sending the payment request:
   println!("[pay] Estimated fee: {estimated_fee_zatoshi} zatoshi ({:.4} ZEC)", estimated_fee_zatoshi as f64 / 1e8);
3. Import zip317_fee and sapling_logical_actions from nie_wallet::fees (or wherever they are re-exported).
4. Keep the existing [pay] requesting ... output line; add the fee line before or after it.

Defensive programming rules:
- The fee estimate is a pre-flight estimate only; actual fee is computed by send_payment() from the real UTXO set. Make this clear in the display: 'Estimated fee (actual may vary based on note selection)'
- Do not pass fee display through any unsafe path; it is purely a UI computation
- Never log key material; this function only logs public data (zatoshi amounts)
- The fee display must not block or error even if wallet is not initialized (use a const estimate in that case)

Agent team: R (read /pay handler lines 1250-1360, identify exact insertion point and imports needed) + I (add fee display) + T (verify output contains fee line in integration test or unit test)

Tests:
- test name: pay_command_displays_fee_estimate — oracle: direct formula computation zip317_fee(sapling_logical_actions(1,2)) = 10000; assertion: output contains '10000 zatoshi' or '0.0001 ZEC'
acceptance_criteria: The /pay command outputs a line containing the estimated fee in zatoshi; cargo test --workspace passes; cargo clippy -D warnings clean; fee display clearly labeled as estimate
